summary: Stage-2 adversarial QC re-opened every load-bearing Arizona citation. A.R.S. 15-213(A)(1), (L), (Q)(3) re-verified verbatim on azleg.gov (5-year multiterm cap with written-board override; IEP private-school-placement carve-out; school-purchasing-cooperative cross-reference to 41-2631). A.R.S. 15-923(A), (B) re-verified verbatim on azleg.gov (transportation contracting authority; silent on term/rebid/renewal; (B) electric-school-bus addition consistent with SB 1246 / Laws 2025 Ch. 242). A.A.C. R7-2-1093(A)-(F) re-verified verbatim on the Cornell LII mirror; the official AZ Secretary of State Title 7 Chapter 2 PDF returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (both lowercase and capitalized path variants); Firecrawl fallback unavailable (MCP token expired) — Stage 1 disclosure of this gap re-confirmed as a genuine, not transient, failure within this audit's tooling envelope. Five of six field verdicts hold; District Variance properly preserved as unresolved (an independent disconfirming sweep on ADE School Finance, Arizona ASBO, AG opinions, and Auditor General materials surfaced no additional credible interpretive-split source). One pinpoint check confirmed A.R.S. 41-2535 (procurement threshold) and 41-2631 (cooperative purchasing definition) both load via the azleg.gov viewdocument route; the cited Title 41 index URL is a generic-portal-not-pinpoint citation as Stage 1 flagged. The Auditor General cited URL 302-redirects to the canonical /resources/school-districts/faqs page (general School Districts FAQ index), not the dedicated procurement subpage its description implies — also a hygiene flag, not load-bearing. One Stage-1 defect dissolved: AZ-C5-01 claimed the Cornell LII URL was missing from research-findings.md Sources Consulted, but it is in fact present at line 277. Internal consistency across scratch/arizona.json, research-findings.md, source-ledger.md, verification-notes.md, and challenge-matrix.md remains strong; the only cross-file artifact is the dependence of R7-2-1093 verification on a non-official mirror, which is uniformly and accurately disclosed in every file. No P1 defect. Five P2/P3 hygiene items remain.

citationVerifications.citation: A.R.S. 15-213(A)(1), (L), (Q)(3) — multiterm 5-year cap with written-board override; enabling statute for School District Procurement Rules; private-school-placement special-ed carve-out at (A)(1); school-purchasing-cooperative cross-reference at (Q)(3)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch re-opened 2026-06-24. (L) verbatim match: 'not more than five years unless the governing board determines in writing before the procurement solicitation is issued that a contract of longer duration would be advantageous'; introductory 'multiterm contracts for materials or services may be entered into if the duration of the contract and the conditions of renewal or extension, if any, are included in the invitation for bids or the request for proposals' also matches. (A)(1) verbatim confirms IEP private-school-placement carve-out (special-ed differentiation). (Q)(3) verbatim confirms 'school purchasing cooperative' definition cross-referenced to 41-2631. Supports max-term, rebid (framework), renewal (mechanics), citation, and special-ed findings.

citationVerifications.citation: A.R.S. 15-923(A), (B) — transportation contracting authority; SB 1246/Laws 2025 Ch. 242 electric-school-bus subsection
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch re-opened 2026-06-24. (A) verbatim: 'A school district may contract for vehicles and other transportation services if found economically advantageous. Contracts may be with another political subdivision, a common or contract carrier or a private party.' Confirmed silent on term, rebid, and renewal — supports the deliverables' conclusion that 15-213(L) and R7-2-1093 (not 15-923) supply those rules. (B) verbatim confirms electric-school-bus preapproved-carrier process tied to the School Bus Advisory Council under 28-3053, consistent with SB 1246 / Laws 2025 Ch. 242.

citationVerifications.claimedTierOrUrl: Tier 2 (State administrative code) — cited URL: https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/arizona/Ariz-Admin-Code-SS-R7-2-1093 (Cornell LII mirror) — official AZ SOS PDF at apps.azsos.gov disclosed as 403
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch re-opened Cornell LII 2026-06-24. Full substantive text re-confirmed: (A) up-front IFB/RFP disclosure + 5-year cap with board override; (B) written governing-board determination that estimated requirements cover the period of the contract and are reasonable and continuing and that the contract promotes competition/economies; (C) non-appropriation cancellation clause; (D) reimbursement for reasonable value of nonrecurring costs not amortized in the price of materials/services delivered; (E) professional services 5-year cap (with exception for projects commenced within that period); (F) auditor contracts governed by A.R.S. 15-213. The official AZ SOS Title 7 Chapter 2 PDF was retried 2026-06-24 with both the lowercase ('title_07') and capitalized ('Title_07') path variants — both returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch; Firecrawl PDF fallback unavailable (Firecrawl MCP token expired). Cornell LII remains a non-official mirror. R7-2-1093 substantive content also corroborated this pass through ASBA model DJE adoption and Tucson Unified Policy DJE adoption (both verified live this pass). Load-bearing finding survives, but primary regulatory verification rests on a non-official mirror — partially verified per audit guardrail.

citationVerifications.citation: A.A.C. R7-2-1001, R7-2-1002, R7-2-1053, R7-2-1055 (Definitions, Applicability, Sole Source, Emergency Procurement)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: HTTP 403 Forbidden on WebFetch 2026-06-24 (both 'title_07' lowercase path as cited and 'Title_07' capitalized variant retrieved from a follow-on search). Firecrawl fallback unavailable this pass (Firecrawl MCP token expired). Stage 1 already surfaced this URL in sourcesUnreachable. Substantive R7-2-1002(D)(8) special-ed carve-out content corroborated indirectly via the WebSearch result quoting the rule text and through the 15-213(A)(1) statutory carve-out re-verified directly at azleg.gov. Not load-bearing in isolation — special-ed differentiation rests on 15-213(A)(1) directly verified. The cited URL itself is also case-mismatched against the path search engines return ('Title_07' capitalized), but the case-corrected URL is also 403 and so the citation remains classified unreachable rather than dead-cited.

WebFetch 2026-06-24. The cited URL renders only Title 41 Chapters 1-9 from the loaded paginated view; Chapter 23 (Procurement Code) and §§ 41-2535 and 41-2631 are not reached from this URL. Pinpoint checks via the azleg.gov viewdocument route succeeded this pass: https://www.azleg.gov/viewdocument/?docName=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.azleg.gov%2Fars%2F41%2F02535.htm loads 41-2535 ('Any procurement that does not exceed the aggregate dollar amount of one hundred thousand dollars may be made in accordance with rules adopted by the director') and https://www.azleg.gov/viewdocument/?docName=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.azleg.gov%2Fars%2F41%2F02631.htm loads 41-2631 (definitions: 'cooperative purchasing means procurement conducted by, or on behalf of, more than one public procurement unit'). The substantive sections exist on azleg.gov at different URLs — confirmed generic-portal-not-pinpoint citation. Cited only in support of cooperative-purchasing and threshold framework; not load-bearing for any of the five fields.

WebFetch re-opened 2026-06-24. Substantive ASBA model policy DJE content loads: Superintendent procurement responsibility per Arizona statutes; bidding exemptions including special-education placements; $10,000 / $100,000 thresholds; 'Contract duration limited to five years unless board determines otherwise.' Consistent with source-ledger description as Tier 5 pathfinder built on A.R.S. 15-213 and 7 A.A.C. Ch. 2 Art. 10.

citationVerifications.citation: Tucson Unified School District Governing Board Policy DJE
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch re-opened 2026-06-24. TUSD Policy DJE loads with verbatim 5-year cap and Board-determination override mirroring 15-213(L) ('shall be limited to no more than five (5) years unless the Board determines that a contract of longer duration would be advantageous to the District'). Matches deliverables' use of TUSD as a large-district variance reference.

citationVerifications.citation: Arizona Auditor General — School Districts Procurement FAQs / FY 2024 State Procurement Compliance Report
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch and follow-on curl 2026-06-24 both show the cited /reports-publications/school-districts/faqs/procurement URL returns HTTP 200 only by way of a server-side redirect (302→200) to the canonical /resources/school-districts/faqs page (H1 'FAQs—School Districts' confirmed). The canonical page is a general School Districts FAQ index containing a 'Procurement' section heading with truncated/non-rendered Q&A content via WebFetch. There is no dedicated /faqs/procurement subpage. The FY 2024 State Procurement Compliance Report (USFR Compliance Questionnaire) referenced in the source-ledger description lives elsewhere on azauditor.gov (Forms—School Districts page). Stage 1 conceded 'WebFetch returned only section headings.' Not load-bearing for any of the five fields; supports District-Variance Unknown only by absence-of-documented-split. Hygiene flag: one URL bundles two artifacts that actually live at different URLs, and the cited URL itself silently redirects to a different page than its description implies.

issues.evidence: Source-ledger row 54 cites https://www.azleg.gov/arsDetail/?title=41 to support A.R.S. Title 41 Ch. 23 and §§ 41-2535, 41-2631. WebFetch 2026-06-24 confirms the URL is a paginated Title 41 index displaying only chapters 1-9; Chapter 23 and §§ 41-2535 / 41-2631 are not reached from this URL. Pinpoint checks confirm 41-2535 exists at https://www.azleg.gov/viewdocument/?docName=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.azleg.gov%2Fars%2F41%2F02535.htm (procurement threshold) and 41-2631 at https://www.azleg.gov/viewdocument/?docName=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.azleg.gov%2Fars%2F41%2F02631.htm (cooperative-purchasing definitions). Generic-portal-not-pinpoint citation. Not load-bearing.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace the cited index URL with pinpoint section URLs for A.R.S. 41-2535 and 41-2631.

issues.field: District Variance / supporting source — Auditor General
issues.evidence: Source-ledger row 57 cites https://www.azauditor.gov/reports-publications/school-districts/faqs/procurement and describes the URL as bundling 'School Districts Procurement FAQs / FY 2024 State Procurement Compliance Report.' Curl 2026-06-24 shows the cited URL returns HTTP 200 only by server-side redirect to https://www.azauditor.gov/resources/school-districts/faqs (canonical 'FAQs—School Districts' index, H1 confirmed). There is no dedicated procurement subpage. The FY 2024 USFR Compliance Questionnaire lives at a different URL on azauditor.gov. Single URL bundles two artifacts and silently redirects to a different page than its description implies. Not load-bearing.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Pinpoint to the canonical https://www.azauditor.gov/resources/school-districts/faqs URL and either (a) split into two ledger rows with a separate row for the FY 2024 USFR Compliance Questionnaire at its actual URL, or (b) trim the description so it matches what loads.

issues.field: Statutory Citation — A.A.C. R7-2-1093 official-source dependency
issues.evidence: Load-bearing regulatory citation A.A.C. R7-2-1093 is verified verbatim only on the Cornell LII mirror (https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/arizona/Ariz-Admin-Code-SS-R7-2-1093). The official Arizona Secretary of State publisher PDF returns HTTP 403 to WebFetch on both the cited lowercase path (https://apps.azsos.gov/public_services/title_07/7-02.pdf) and the capitalized variant returned by search engines (https://apps.azsos.gov/public_services/Title_07/7-02.pdf). Firecrawl fallback unavailable this pass (Firecrawl MCP plugin requires re-authorization). Substantive text further corroborated this pass against TUSD Policy DJE and ASBA model DJE adoption. Stage 1 already disclosed the 403; nevertheless, primary regulatory verification rests on a non-official mirror.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add an alternative official-or-state-sanctioned source for R7-2-1093 if locatable (e.g., the Arizona Administrative Register filing that originally promulgated the 2014/2018 amendments, a State Procurement Office reprint at spo.az.gov, or a future successful Firecrawl PDF parse after MCP re-authorization). Otherwise retain existing disclosure and the verification-fallback note that the text was corroborated against TUSD/ASBA policy adoption.

issues.evidence: research-findings.md line 29 table cell paraphrases 15-213(L) without the leading 'Unless otherwise provided by law,' qualifier that appears verbatim in the live statute. Substantive meaning preserved; nuance (which preserves any superseding statute-specific exception) dropped.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optional: prepend 'Unless otherwise provided by law' or add 'subject to any contrary statute' to the table cell. Pure verbatim-fidelity hygiene; not material to the legal claim.

issues.field: Adversarial robustness — District Variance challenge row strengthening
issues.evidence: Challenge-matrix row for District Variance (challenge-matrix.md line 36 area) is a real (not pro forma) challenge, querying azag.gov, azauditor.gov, ASBA, and TUSD/Deer Valley adoptions. This audit's independent disconfirming sweep on Arizona ASBO bulletins, ADE School Finance Office, and additional district policy adoptions located no further credible official Arizona statewide-uniformity or interpretive-split source; one Heinfeld Meech procurement-FAQ secondary source surfaced but is not Tier 1-4 authority. The 'Unknown' verdict is appropriate. Strengthening opportunity only.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Optionally add an ADE School Finance Office and Arizona ASBO bulletin sweep notation to the District Variance challenge-matrix entry. Not required — current row meets the 'real challenge' bar.

issues.field: Sources Consulted — Arizona section completeness (Stage 1 defect re-checked and dissolved)
issues.evidence: Stage 1 AZ-C5-01 asserted that research-findings.md 'Sources Consulted' Arizona list does not name the Cornell LII mirror for R7-2-1093. Re-read 2026-06-24: research-findings.md line 277 reads '- A.A.C. R7-2-1093 — https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/arizona/Ariz-Admin-Code-SS-R7-2-1093', i.e., the Cornell LII URL IS present in Sources Consulted. Stage 1's evidence was factually incorrect. Defect dissolved; retained here as a -DISSOLVED marker for audit traceability — no remediation required.
issues.recommendedRemediation: No action — defect dissolved on re-read of research-findings.md line 277.
